title: 'We are walking a fine line': Incurably ill patients on France's new assisted-dying law
subhead: A law creating a right to aid in dying was adopted by French lawamkers on Wednesday. Le Monde spoke with people facing serious illness about how the choice weighs on their lives.

title: French parliament adopts assisted dying law supported by Macron
France facing 'very worrying' drought, says environment minister
Sébastien Lecornu visits Morocco to strengthen ties between Paris and Rabat
subhead: Accompanied by 12 ministers, the prime minister is traveling to Rabat for his first official trip abroad on July 15-16. The visit offers an opportunity to deepen bilateral cooperation with the Moroccan authorities.

title: René Obermann, chairman of Airbus: 'We must put an end to industrial nationalism in defense'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde and Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung, the chair of the board of the European aerospace and defense group expressed concern about the future of French-German cooperation, which has been undermined by the cancellation in June of the FCAS, the next-generation fighter jet project.

title: Argentina beat England 2-1 to reach World Cup 2026 final
subhead: Argentina will have a chance to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to win back-to-back World Cups after Lionel Messi, 39, once again dragged his team to victory.

title: 2026 World Cup: How France's American dream built up speed – and crashed
subhead: France fell hard to Spain on Tuesday in the semifinals of the football tournament, marking a cruel and unexpected ending for Didier Deschamps' squad, who had delighted supporters since the start of the competition.

'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
subhead: After months of silence, two men suspected of stealing the royal jewels described their involvement in detail during new interrogations in June. According to their statements, they were hired to carry out the heist for '€15,000 to €25,000.'

title: 'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
subhead: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

title: 'Thank you, dear Hungary, for proving that anything is always possible'
subhead: Jean-Baptiste Chastand, Le Monde's Central Europe correspondent, writes a letter to this 'hostile and surprising' country he has covered for more than 10 years.
